bugs.title: graph-schema.json description in README is misleading
bugs.evidence: README 'What's New in v2.0' section describes graph-schema.json as 'a formal JSON Schema at .code-atlas/graph-schema.json that describes the full shape of both atlas.json and state.json'. This is factually incorrect.
bugs.expected: graph-schema.json is the semantic dependency graph containing annotated nodes (modules/files with roles, criticality, stability, test_coverage) and edges (dependencies with type, strength, directionality, impact). It is not a JSON Schema descriptor.
bugs.suggested_fix: Replace the misleading description with: 'v2.0 generates a semantic dependency graph at .code-atlas/graph-schema.json with annotated modules/files (roles, criticality, stability, test_coverage) and dependency edges (type, strength, directionality, impact). Use /code-atlas:query to interrogate it.'

bugs.title: README query types table omits transitive_dependents operation
bugs.evidence: The 'Supported query types' table in 'What's New in v2.0' lists only filter, dependencies_of, and dependents_of. The fourth operation transitive_dependents is fully implemented and documented in query-executor.ts and query/SKILL.md but missing from README table.
bugs.expected: All four supported query operations should be listed in the README's query types table.
bugs.suggested_fix: Add row to query types table: | `transitive_dependents` | List all modules that transitively depend on the given file (upstream consumer set) |

summary: Wave 2: 5 agents verified clean (graph-generator, query-executor, schema-validator, query skill, update skill docs). 1 agent failed (README): 2 documentation bugs found. Both are in the README.md file created by wave-2-agent-6. 1 P1 (graph-schema.json description factually incorrect) and 1 P2 (transitive_dependents operation missing from table).
